title: pimelea microcephala subsp. glabra
intro: Pimelea microcephala subsp. glabra, also known as Smooth Rice Flower or Glabrous Rice Flower, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its small white or pale yellow flowers. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Pimelea microcephala subsp. glabra include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or oil to create a topical treatment or infusion for medicinal use.
